name: BFSS matrix theory (M-theory as a matrix model)
summary: Conjectured non-perturbative definition of M-theory in the infinite-momentum frame as the N -> infinity limit of supersymmetric matrix quantum mechanics describing N D0-branes. Reproduces the full Fock space of 11-dimensional supergravity as bound states of D0-branes, and provides a non-perturbative realisation of the holographic principle. Foundational alongside AdS/CFT for the modern non-perturbative formulation of string/M-theory.

addresses_puzzles.note: Provides a non-perturbative definition of M-theory in which BH states are constructible as matrix-model configurations; the matrix model is unitary by construction, so BH evaporation preserves information. Generalisation to asymptotically-flat 4D BHs remains an active research goal.
Conjectured non-perturbative definition of M-theory as the N -> infinity limit of supersymmetric matrix quantum mechanics in the infinite-momentum frame (Banks-Fischler-Shenker-Susskind 1997).

touches_benchmarks.note: 11D supergravity emerges as the IR limit; 4D GR follows from compactification.

new_fields: N x N matrices in supersymmetric matrix quantum mechanics
introduces.note: Matrix size N (taken to infinity) and 't Hooft coupling. The matrix model is itself parameter-free in the N -> infinity limit.

history: Banks, Fischler, Shenker, Susskind (1996), 'M theory as a matrix model: a conjecture', Phys. Rev. D 55, 5112 (1997), arXiv:hep-th/9610043. Extensive tests through the late 1990s reproduced supergravity scattering amplitudes. Less actively studied since AdS/CFT became the dominant non-perturbative tool, but provides an independent non-perturbative definition.

references.citation: Banks, Fischler, Shenker, Susskind, 'M theory as a matrix model: a conjecture', Phys. Rev. D 55, 5112 (1997)
